Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

3. Early access to home modifications and equipment - a separate upfront fund of up to $15,000 for assistive technology and home modifications, rather than having to save up your package funds.

4. Care management fees to be capped at 10% of the participants quarterly budget. 

While we’re excited about the much overdue changes to packages and waitlists we’re concerned that some providers will have to make up for lost care management revenue by increasing YOUR service fees. Because of this its more important then ever to be vigilant and understand your management fees AND hourly service fees.

We've seen service fees vary by up to 100% in the same region. Meaning that even though management fees might be lower, people still aren't getting as much care as they are entitled to.

We've built a free tool that compares providers by management fees and service fees. All you have to do is enter your postcode and your home care package level and we'll show you the best value for money providers in your area.

https://www.caringco.com.au/app/homecare
Don't set and forget your Home Care Package Provider

With Australia’s biggest aged care reform in nearly three decades now live, thousands of older Australians are discovering changes they didn’t expect — from fees shifting, to services being repriced, to support levels being reassessed.

If you’re wondering whether you’re still with the right provider, you’re asking the right question.

Because under Support at Home, every dollar matters — and things like unclear communication, unexplained fees, or support plans that don’t reflect your needs can quickly add up.

The good news?

Switching providers is easier than most people think — most families transition in 2–4 weeks. And with service prices now varying by as much as 100%, choosing a better-value provider could mean twice as much care from the same budget.

You deserve care that feels transparent, fair, and truly centred around you 💚

If you’re deciding how to manage a Home Care Package, here’s a gentle breakdown to help you breathe a little easier.

💚 Care-Managed
Think of this option as having a dedicated co-pilot. Your approved provider assigns a Care Manager who organises every service—from personal care and cleaning to nursing and physio. They handle rosters, paperwork, quality checks, and any hiccups along the way.

✅ Less admin stress: The provider pays invoices, keeps the budget on track, and stays on top of government reporting.

✅ Clinical peace of mind: Ideal if Mum or Dad has complex medical needs or you’re already stretched thin.

❓Cost trade-off: A higher “care-management” fee comes out of the package, which means slightly fewer funds for hands-on care.

💜 Self-Managed
If you prefer rolling up your sleeves, self-management gives you the driver’s seat. Your provider still keeps the package compliant in the background, but you (or a trusted family member) hire workers, set schedules, and approve spending.

✅ More choice, more control: Hand-pick carers and negotiate rates that suit your budget.

✅ Lower ongoing fees: Savings on care-management fees free up dollars for extra hours of support.

❓Added responsibility: You’ll handle timesheets, invoices, and day-to-day problem-solving.

✨Which path feels right?
There’s no “one-size-fits-all.” Some families cherish the freedom of self-management; others value the relief of professional oversight. Whatever you choose, remember you’re not alone—Caring Co’s friendly team is here to answer questions, share real-world tips, and walk alongside you every step of the way.
Don't set and forget your Home Care Package Provider

Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

3. Early access to home modifications and equipment - a separate upfront fund of up to $15,000 for assistive technology and home modifications, rather than having to save up your package funds.

4. Care management fees to be capped at 10% of the participants quarterly budget. 

While we’re excited about the much overdue changes to packages and waitlists we’re concerned that some providers will have to make up for lost care management revenue by increasing YOUR service fees. Because of this its more important then ever to be vigilant and understand your management fees AND hourly service fees.

We've seen service fees vary by up to 100% in the same region. Meaning that even though management fees might be lower, people still aren't getting as much care as they are entitled to.

We've built a free tool that compares providers by management fees and service fees. All you have to do is enter your postcode and your home care package level and we'll show you the best value for money providers in your area.

https://www.caringco.com.au/app/homecare
Don't set and forget your Home Care Package Provider

Same service, double the price? Check your Support at Home rates

With Australia’s biggest aged care reform in nearly three decades now live, thousands of older Australians are discovering changes they didn’t expect — from fees shifting, to services being repriced, to support levels being reassessed.

If you’re wondering whether you’re still with the right provider, you’re asking the right question.

Because under Support at Home, every dollar matters — and things like unclear communication, unexplained fees, or support plans that don’t reflect your needs can quickly add up.

The good news?

Switching providers is easier than most people think — most families transition in 2–4 weeks. And with service prices now varying by as much as 100%, choosing a better-value provider could mean twice as much care from the same budget.

You deserve care that feels transparent, fair, and truly centred around you 💚

This background has provided him with advanced skills in surgical technique, wound management, and the aesthetic outcomes of skin procedures.
He later completed General Practice via a prestigious NIHR Academic Clinical Fellowship, combining surgical expertise with comprehensive, evidence-based primary care.
David holds an award-winning PhD (DPhil) from the University of Oxford, has published extensively in medical literature, and has presented his research globally. He has also taught at leading institutions, including Imperial College London and the University of Oxford. In practice, David places a strong emphasis on early diagnosis and high-quality procedural care.
He is known for his thorough consultations and focus on preventative health strategies -

If you’ve ever tried booking with Glo Dental Clinic, you might have noticed something.

There are two clinics listed.

And naturally a small question pops into your mind: “Which one am I supposed to pick?” 🤔

Many patients mention having the exact same thought, especially when they’re visiting for the first time.

It’s a completely normal moment of confusion when you realise there are two locations to choose from.

The two clinics are actually only about five minutes apart, and both are run by the same team with the same approach to patient care.

So whichever location you choose, you’ll still be seeing the same familiar team.

🦷 Cockburn Central — Plaza 817 on Beeliar Drive  
🦷 Beeliar — Beeliar Drive in Yangebup  
🦷 The same team and philosophy across both locations  
🦷 Convenient parking and the same payment options

Because the clinics are so close together, many people simply choose the location that fits best with their day.

For some, it’s the one closer to home. For others, it might be the appointment time that works better with their schedule.

If you’d like to organise a visit, you’re welcome to book at whichever Glo Dental Clinic is most convenient for you. 🤍

Because when I look back at that year with my daughter, I realize I made it SO much harder than it needed to be.

With my first, I was understandably a little arrogant. I thought "how hard can it be?"

Oh boy was I wrong....

And that was the decision that wasted nearly a year of our lives.

I’d ask "Did you just go?" and she’d say "No."

And I thought she was lying straight to my face.

I spent months begging, bribing, and pleading for her to sit on the potty.

I thought she was stubborn. I thought she "wasn't ready." I thought I was failing her.

She did eventually get it. One day she just decided she wanted to use the toilet. Like... Excuse me??? 

All this time just for you to decide?? 🤦🤦🤦

So when my son turned 2, I felt dread. Not excitement. Dread.

I looked at my husband and said: "I cannot go through this again with him."

It started at 33. The exhaustion that sleep couldn't fix. The fog that swallowed every thought before I could finish it. The night I screamed at my four-year-old over a glass of spilled juice and saw actual fear in his eyes. I spent 3 years convinced I was just stressed. Turns out I was right — just not in the way I thought.

At 33, you don't think "burnout."

You think: I'm just tired. Work is crazy. The kids are young. Everyone feels like this.

So I pushed through. Like we do. Coffee before sunrise, emails after midnight, and a lot of "I'm good" in between.

But I wasn't good.

The exhaustion got worse. Not "I need a vacation" tired. "I could quit everything and sleep for a year and still wake up empty" tired.

Then came the fog. Not forgetfulness. A total whiteout. I'd stand in the middle of a sentence in a meeting and forget what I was saying. I'd drive to the grocery store and sit in the parking lot because I couldn't remember what we needed. My wife would ask me a simple question and I'd stare at her like she was speaking another language.

And then — the thing that scared me most.

I stopped feeling anything.

Not sad. Not angry. Not happy. Just... flat. Like someone pulled the plug on whatever makes a person a person.

My kids would run to me screaming "DADDY!" and I'd hug them and feel nothing. I'd go through the motions — smile, ruffle their hair, say "hey buddy" — but inside it was like watching a movie of someone else's life.

At 33. Married to a woman I loved. Father to kids I'd die for. And suddenly feeling nothing for any of it.

34 was the year of excuses.

Maybe I need more sleep? Bought a better mattress. Tried melatonin. Still woke up at 3 AM staring at the ceiling, running through tomorrow's problems on repeat.

Maybe it's the job? Took a week off. Felt the same. Maybe worse, because now I didn't even have w

Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

While we’re excited about the much overdue changes to packages and waitlists we’re concerned that some providers will have to make up for lost care management revenue by increasing YOUR service fees. We've seen service fees vary by up to 100% in the same region. Meaning that even though management fees might be lower, people still aren't getting as much care as they are entitled to.

We've built a free tool that compares providers by management fees and service fees. All you have to do is enter your postcode and your home care package level and we'll show you the best value for money providers in your area.

Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

3. Early access to home modifications and equipment - a separate upfront fund of up to $15,000 for assistive technology and home modifications, rather than having to save up your package funds.

4. Care management fees to be capped at 10% of the participants quarterly budget. 

While we’re excited about the much overdue changes to packages and waitlists we’re concerned that some providers will have to make up for lost care management revenue by increasing YOUR service fees. Because of this its more important then ever to be vigilant and understand your management fees AND hourly service fees.

We've seen service fees vary by up to 100% in the same region. Meaning that even though management fees might be lower, people still aren't getting as much care as they are entitled to.

We've built a free tool that compares providers by management fees and service fees. All you have to do is enter your postcode and your home care package level and we'll show you the best value for money providers in your area.

https://www.caringco.com.au/app/homecare
Don't set and forget your Home Care Package Provider
